headline: Ross Gerber Warns Inflation's Persistence Diminishes Market Optimism For Stocks And Bonds
summary: On Saturday, Gerber Kawasaki Wealth & Investment Management CEO Ross Gerber, said market selling pressure is starting to build, arguing it has become difficult to stay optimistic while inflation remains stubborn.

headline: As US-Israel Campaign Intensifies With Iran's Nuclear Facility Strike, Global Energy Markets Face Critical Juncture, Israel Vows to Intensify Attack
summary: Iran's atomic energy organization on Saturday said that the United States and Israel struck the Natanz enrichment complex, a major uranium-processing site in central Iran, while Iranian officials reported no off-site radiation release or danger to nearby residents.

headline: Elon Musk Offers To Cover TSA Salaries Amid Funding Crisis: 'Negatively Affecting Lives of So Many Americans'
summary: Elon Musk has said that he wants to cover Transportation Security Administration paychecks while a federal funding lapse leaves airport screeners working without salaries.

headline: Yelp Manager Turns Vacation Joke Into A Serious Side Hustle That Prints More Than $125,000 In Sales In Just Over A Year
summary: Joey Coffin turned an inside joke into a successful side-hustle by starting small, testing the market, and eventually quitting his job to focus on his business.

headline: Steve Ballmer Once Recalled Charlie Munger Telling Him: 'I Know, You're Not That Smart' — Here's What The Ex-Microsoft CEO Replied
summary: Steve Ballmer recalled how Charlie Munger once mocked his decision to hold Microsoft stock, a loyalty-driven bet that ultimately helped build his $125 billion fortune.

headline: Benzinga Bulls And Bears: FedEx, Micron, SoFi — And Markets Volatile As Iran War Continues
summary: Benzinga examined the prospects for many investors' favorite stocks over the last week — here's a look at some of our top stories.

headline: EXCLUSIVE: 'Intermediate US Treasury Duration Attractive': BondBloxx Honcho Explains Why Investors Are Moving Into 2-Year Treasuries
summary: Senator John Boozman's XTWO ETF purchase highlights a shift to 2-year duration as investors seek yield amid unclear Fed policy outlook, says BondBloxx.

headline: Scale AI Launches 'Voice Showdown' To Rival OpenAI, xAI, Anthropic In Voice AI Models
summary: Scale AI has released Voice Showdown, a platform for evaluating voice AI models, as it seeks to compete with its rivals .

headline: Super Micro Computer, Micron Technology And More: 5 Stocks Investors Couldn't Stop Buzzing About This Week
summary: Retail investors talked up five hot stocks this week (March 9 to March 13) on X and Reddit's r/WallStreetBets: SMCI, MU, ULTA, CF, NVDA.

headline: UN Chief Backs Gaza Reconstruction Plan Linked To Trump But Rejects Broader Agenda— 'Cooperating Actively'
summary: UN chief Antonio Guterres backed Trump's Gaza reconstruction efforts but rejected his broader agenda, urging UN-led solutions for the Strait of Hormuz amid rising Iran tensions and escalating war costs.

headline: Reddit Weighs Face ID And Passkeys To Verify 'Humans'— CEO Alexis Ohanian Admits 'Just Don't Know How To...'
summary: Reddit is exploring Face ID and passkey-based verification to ensure users are human amid rising bot concerns, though co-founder Alexis Ohanian has raised doubts about user acceptance of face-scanning.

headline: Judge Strikes Down Rules Allowing Press Credential Revocations: 'First Amendment... Must Not Be Abandoned Now'
summary: A federal judge struck down key parts of the Pentagon's press policy, ruling that revoking journalists' credentials over their reporting violates First Amendment protections and ordering access to be restored.

headline: United Airlines Slashes Flights By 5% As Iran War Spikes Fuel Costs— CEO Scott Kirby Warns Of $175 Oil Scenario
summary: United Airlines is cutting 5% of its flights and bracing for a prolonged fuel cost surge driven by the Iran conflict, even as strong travel demand allows the airline to raise fares and cushion the financial impact.

headline: Super Micro Co-Founder Exits Board As Nvidia Chip Smuggling Indictment Batters Stock
summary: Super Micro Computer Inc's co-founder, Yih-Shyan Wally Liaw, resigned from the board after being indicted for smuggling Nvidia AI chips into China.

headline: Trump Says US Is 'Considering Winding Down' Iran War But Rejects Ceasefire As Khamenei Claims Enemies Are 'Defeated'
summary: Trump says US is close to achieving objectives Iran war but rejects ceasefire as Strait of Hormuz disruptions drive oil prices higher while stock futures fall.

headline: Palantir Scores Big As Pentagon Reportedly Adopts Maven AI Across All Military Branches
summary: Pentagon reportedly adopts Palantir's Maven as official program of record, securing long-term funding for AI weapons-targeting platform used in military strikes.

headline: Elon Musk Found Liable By Jury For Misleading Twitter Investors In $44 Billion Deal, Faces Potential $2.5 Billion Damages
summary: A jury found Elon Musk liable for misleading Twitter investors over bot claims in the $44 billion deal, risking up to $2.5 billion in damages.
